name: Amrut
summary: Bengaluru's Amrut was India's first single malt to win global acclaim, kick-starting the country's premium whisky story.
angle: How a regional distiller broke into a market dominated by Scotch and reframed 'Indian whisky' as premium.

name: Paul John
summary: Goa-made Paul John single malts have collected international awards, riding India's tropical maturation advantage.
angle: Why India's climate matures whisky faster — and how that becomes a marketing and margin story.

name: Indri
summary: Haryana's Indri became a breakout Indian single malt, winning top honours at global whisky awards.
angle: The rapid rise of a new premium brand and what awards do to pricing power.

name: Rampur
summary: Rampur is a luxury Indian single malt aimed at global markets and duty-free shelves.
angle: Premiumization at the top end — how a legacy distiller built a luxury export brand.

name: Godawan
summary: Godawan is Diageo India's artisanal single malt, a big-player bet on the premium Indian whisky wave.
angle: When a multinational enters craft — premiumization from the top down.

name: McDowell's No.1
summary: One of India's highest-selling spirits brands by volume, spanning whisky and rum.
angle: The economics of scale — how a mass brand wins on distribution and price, not prestige.

name: Royal Stag
summary: A huge-selling Indian whisky famous for its 'Make It Large' branding and surrogate music/CD campaigns.
angle: Surrogate advertising in action — selling 'music' because you can't advertise the whisky.

name: Blenders Pride
summary: A premium-segment Indian whisky built on aspirational, status-led branding.
angle: How branding manufactures 'premium' perception in a crowded whisky market.

name: Officer's Choice
summary: One of the world's largest-selling whisky brands by volume, built on the value end of the market.
angle: Value economics — winning the mass market on price and reach.

name: Imperial Blue
summary: A high-volume value whisky known for its humour-led surrogate 'Men Will Be Men' campaigns.
angle: Memorable surrogate advertising that built brand recall without showing the product.

name: Old Monk
summary: India's iconic dark rum, famous for near-zero advertising and a devoted cult following.
angle: The anti-marketing brand — how word-of-mouth alone built a legend.

name: Kingfisher
summary: India's dominant beer brand and a textbook surrogate-advertising case via packaged water and events.
angle: How Kingfisher stayed top-of-mind through surrogate branding and sponsorships.

name: Bira 91
summary: A craft-styled beer brand that made premium, design-led beer aspirational for young urban India.
angle: Design and positioning as the product — how Bira premiumised mass beer.

name: Simba
summary: An Indian craft beer brand competing on flavour and identity against legacy lagers.
angle: The craft-beer margin play in a market built on strong lager.

name: Sula
summary: Sula effectively built India's modern wine category and wine-tourism economy in Nashik.
angle: Creating a category from scratch — and monetising it through tourism, not just bottles.

name: Fratelli
summary: A premium Indian wine label helping push domestic wine upmarket.
angle: Premiumization in a nascent category with thin per-capita consumption.

name: Magic Moments
summary: India's leading vodka brand, built on flavoured variants and youthful branding.
angle: How flavour innovation captured a young, first-time-spirits audience.

name: Greater Than
summary: One of India's first craft gins, part of the country's craft-spirits movement.
angle: The craft-gin wave — small batches, high margins, urban positioning.

name: Hapusa
summary: A Himalayan-juniper craft gin that leaned into Indian botanicals and terroir.
angle: Terroir as a premium story — selling 'Indian-ness' at a higher price.

name: Signature
summary: A premium-segment whisky using aspirational, lifestyle-led branding.
angle: Lifestyle branding as the differentiator in a sea of similar whiskies.
